evals.prompt: We just deployed a React SPA with a Node.js Express backend. Our security scanner flagged missing security headers and insecure cookie configuration. Can you audit our frontend security setup against ASVS V3? We use a CDN for some JavaScript libraries and have a CORS setup for our API.
evals.expected_output: The audit should check for the presence and correctness of all security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy), verify session cookies have Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite flags set with __Host- prefix recommended, evaluate the CSP for unsafe-inline or unsafe-eval directives and recommend nonce-based script loading, check CORS configuration for wildcard origins especially with credentials, verify all CDN-loaded scripts and stylesheets have SRI integrity attributes, and check that auth tokens are not stored in localStorage. Should recommend Helmet.js for Express header management and provide specific configuration examples.

evals.prompt: Our web application loads user-generated content including rich text, uploaded images, and embedded iframes. We've had a report of a clickjacking vulnerability and want to make sure our CORS and content isolation are properly configured. Audit our frontend against ASVS V3.4 and V3.5 requirements.
evals.expected_output: The audit should verify X-Frame-Options DENY or CSP frame-ancestors 'none' is set to prevent clickjacking, check that user-uploaded content (especially SVG and HTML) is served from a separate domain or with Content-Disposition: attachment, evaluate CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin for overly permissive configuration and ensure origin validation uses exact matching not substring matching, verify CSRF protection is implemented on all state-changing endpoints, check SameSite cookie attributes for defense-in-depth against CSRF, and review iframe sandboxing for embedded content. Should provide specific header configurations and CORS allowlist validation code examples.
